title: Back Up a Volume
description:
## The Classic Volume Backup Pattern

Named volumes are managed by Docker, so you back them up by mounting the volume **and** a host directory into a throwaway container that tars one into the other.

A volume named **`bkp-vol`** has been prepared with some data in it.

**Your task:** produce a backup archive at **`/root/backup.tar`** containing the contents of `bkp-vol`.

> 💡 Run a helper container that mounts the volume and your home directory, then tars the volume's contents:
> `docker run --rm -v bkp-vol:/data -v /root:/backup alpine tar cf /backup/backup.tar -C /data .`

hints.title: Mount both the volume and a target dir
hints.body: A throwaway container can see the named volume and a host path at the same time, letting tar copy between them.
hints.command: docker run --rm -v bkp-vol:/data -v /root:/backup alpine tar cf /backup/backup.tar -C /data .

hints.title: Inspect the archive
hints.body: List the archive contents to confirm the volume's files were captured.

setup_commands.command: docker run --rm -v bkp-vol:/data alpine sh -c 'echo backup-me > /data/file.txt'

validation.description: Checks that /root/backup.tar exists and contains the volume's file.

commands.description: Archive contains the volume's data file
